note: Linked Field has no plugin types, no .api.php, and no central linking UI — it adds a formatter third-party setting applied at render by a hook + one service. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded): they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I installed Linked Field. Where and how do I make an image field link to another URL — is there a settings form for it?
evals.expected_output: There is no central linking UI; on Manage display open the field's formatter settings and tick the 'Link this field' checkbox, then choose the destination.

evals.prompt: How does Linked Field store the per-field link configuration, and how does it inject the 'Link this field' checkbox into a formatter?
evals.expected_output: It stores the config as formatter third-party settings (namespace linked_field) on the display component, added via hook_field_formatter_third_party_settings_form().

evals.prompt: What destination types can Linked Field's 'Link this field' offer, and what does each mean?
evals.expected_output: Type 'field' uses another field's value on the same entity as the href; type 'custom' is a URL/path string that supports tokens.

evals.prompt: For a Linked Field custom destination, how do I enter an internal path like node 1, and can I use tokens such as [node:url]?
evals.expected_output: Enter /node/1, node/1, or internal:/node/1 (bare paths get an internal:/ prefix); tokens like [node:url] are supported when the Token module is enabled.

evals.prompt: Which link attributes does Linked Field expose by default (target, rel, etc.), and where do I change the set of available attributes?
evals.expected_output: Defaults are title, target, class, rel (from linked_field.config); edit the YAML attributes map at /admin/config/linked_field/config (route linked_field.config, permission 'administer linked_field').

evals.prompt: At render time, what applies the link to a field's output in Linked Field, and which service builds the URL and wraps the markup?
evals.expected_output: hook_entity_display_build_alter() applies it, using the linked_field.manager service (buildDestinationUrl() for the URL, linkHtml() to wrap the markup in an <a>).

evals.prompt: Can I use Linked Field on a link-type field? Why or why not?
evals.expected_output: No — link-type fields are blacklisted (getFieldTypeBlacklist() returns ['link']) since they already render links; the 'Link this field' option is not offered for them.

evals.prompt: In Linked Field, how do I make the link text something other than the field's own output?
evals.expected_output: Use the Advanced → Text setting (a token-capable string); when set it overrides the field's rendered output as the link text.
